title: Asynchronous Architecture and Pattern Selection · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective pattern selection and coupling decision record; preserve message identity and timing, locate the first broken ownership boundary, and retain a deterministic regression case. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in pattern selection and coupling decision record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evide…
objectives: Choose asynchronous messaging only when temporal decoupling, buffering, fan-out, failure isolation, or replay creates measurable value.
Distinguish competing consumers on one queue from publish/subscribe using one queue per subscriber responsibility.
Evaluate request/reply over messaging against ordinary HTTP using correlation, timeout, cancellation, failure ambiguity, and capacity evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Order the regression proof · Debug Broker Fit Boundary
steps.instruction:
Order the Broker Fit Boundary RabbitMQ and TypeScript HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Order the regression proof · Debug Broker Fit Boundary.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Broker Fit Boundary assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Order the regression proof · Debug Broker Fit Boundary, keep Broker Fit Boundary connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.text: A debug messaging team handles case 3474 in a privacy-sensitive audit fanout with an offline subscriber. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose HTTP, work queue, pub/sub, or retained stream from explicit coupling needs; keep every queue and subscriber responsibility explicitly named; then reuse one message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare RabbitMQ queues, streams, databases, job schedulers, and logs against ordering, retention, replay, routing, throughput, and operational constraints.

Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare RabbitMQ queues, streams, databases, job schedulers, and logs against ordering, retention, replay, routing, throughput, and operational constraints.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Broker Fit Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: RabbitMQ queues are a durable event log and should retain all history indefinitely.
